emissionMargins.description: Per-frequency worst-case emission margins so a downstream gate review or reliability qualification can cite the EMC result structurally rather than from prose.
emissionMargins.schema: array<{ frequencyMHz: number, limitDbuV: number, measuredDbuV: number, marginDb: number, detector: string, pass: boolean }>

criteria.criterion: PASS if the worst-case radiated-emissions margin is computed as (limit − measured) and compared against the ≥4 dB CISPR 32 Class B hard gate (≥6 dB for Class A). FAIL if the gate is misstated, mis-applied, or the comparison is wrong.
PASS if the pass/fail determination is evaluated on the worst-case production unit corresponding to the 3σ statistical production boundary, not a single golden sample. FAIL otherwise.

PASS if the stated PASS/FAIL disposition is logically consistent with the measured margin against the applicable limit at every frequency. FAIL if a unit that misses the limit is reported as passing, or vice versa.
PASS if the radiated scan covers 30 MHz–6 GHz per CISPR 32 Table 1 and reports every frequency where emissions exceed 80% of the limit. FAIL if the range or the near-limit points are incomplete.
PASS if peak and quasi-peak detector readings are reported at all frequencies exceeding 80% of the limit, and quasi-peak is used for the final compliance determination. FAIL otherwise.

PASS if the report names the test facility, confirms a CISPR 16-1-4 site-validated semi-anechoic chamber (3 m or 10 m), and confirms the lab holds current IEC/ISO 17025 accreditation covering the relevant standards. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the equipment-under-test revision, operating mode, cabling, and support equipment are documented so the measurement is reproducible. FAIL if the tested configuration is ambiguous.

PASS if the emissions class (CISPR 32 Class A vs Class B) is correctly selected for the product's market and the corresponding margin requirement is applied; Class A designation, if used, is recorded as approved. FAIL otherwise.

PASS if a positive margin below the 4 dB target is explicitly flagged for monitoring rather than silently reported as a clean pass. FAIL if a marginal result is presented as fully compliant with no caveat.
PASS if, for any failure, the report initiates the Section 9 disposition path — root-cause analysis and corrective action — rather than closing the failure without follow-up. FAIL otherwise.

PASS if the report cites the governing EMC Test and Compliance SOP (SUM-EMC-SOP-005) as the source of the pass/fail criteria. FAIL if the hard gate is asserted with no reference to the controlling procedure.
PASS if the report identifies the exact schematic/BOM revision of the unit under test (e.g. SUM-TAH-SCH-001 / the RevC1 BOM). FAIL if the hardware revision is not traceable.

PASS if a retest report references the prior failing baseline report (e.g. SUM-TAH-EMC-001) and the intervening root-cause analysis (SUM-TAH-EMC-002) that justified the design change. FAIL if a retest stands alone with no link to the failure it clears.
PASS if the 4 dB (or 6 dB) margin value is attributed to the Stonefield Threshold Registry (SUM-REG-THR-001), not just to prose in the SOP. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if any EMC risk the report closes or affects is cited by its risk-register identifier (e.g. R-002 in SUM-PGM-RSK-001). FAIL if a risk state change is asserted without the register reference.

PASS if every regulatory clause, standard, SOP, procedure, and controlled-document identifier cited in the output appears in the retrieved sources. FAIL if any citation is invented or cannot be traced.
PASS if every measurement, margin, count, score, and figure in the output traces to the retrieved data, tables, or tool results. FAIL if any number is unsupported.
PASS if the output contains every section the governing procedure requires for a EMC test report. FAIL if a required section is missing or empty.
PASS if every factual claim in the output is supported by the retrieved sources and the exact data provided. FAIL otherwise.
PASS if the output is written clearly and unambiguously, appropriate for a controlled quality record, with correct document and revision identifiers. FAIL otherwise.

trajectory.description: Provenance checks against the recorded retrieval trajectory for an EMC test report.

required.reason: The pass/fail hard gate (≥4 dB CISPR 32 Class B margin) is defined by SUM-EMC-SOP-005; an EMC report produced without it has no authority for its determination.
The margin threshold is registered in SUM-REG-THR-001; the report's limit values must trace there structurally rather than being restated from memory.

forbidden.reason: A current determination must not be grounded in a superseded/closed EMC report revision as if it were live evidence.
